verdicts.note: Evidence confirms this is the Google Ads API.
Multiple evidence URLs confirm developers.google.com/google-ads as the correct domain.
Google Ads is clearly a marketing and advertising platform.
Evidence confirms REST/gRPC access, OAuth2 + developer-token auth, free API, and manager account/access-level approval process.
Evidence explicitly documents OAuth 2.0 requirement, developer-token header, login-customer-id header for manager accounts, service account support, and multi-user OAuth workflow support.
Evidence confirms developer token requires manager account setup and access level application/approval (Basic/Standard), not instant self-serve.
Evidence confirms API is free, developer token obtained via manager account, and access level approval process (RMF compliance for Standard access).
Evidence confirms REST API exists (developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest), plus gRPC and GAQL query language, with broad campaign management and reporting coverage.

The record claims exists='yes' with URL 'https://github.com/googleads/google-ads-mcp', but the provided evidence contains no mention of any Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. The evidence only covers the Google Ads API documentation itself. Without evidence of a dedicated MCP server, this must be marked 'unknown'.
No evidence provided for the existence of an MCP server at the claimed GitHub URL. The evidence URLs are all official Google Ads API docs, not MCP server repos.
Evidence supports 'buildable-with-caveats' due to the developer token approval process requiring manager account setup and access level application.

Evidence confirms developer token approval process (manager account + access level application) is required. The claim about official MCP server being read-only is unsupported by evidence but the first part of the blocker is accurate.
All evidence URLs are valid Google Ads API documentation pages that support the claims made.
0.8 is reasonable given the comprehensive evidence for most fields, though MCP server claims lack evidence.
First flag (requires_developer_token_approval) is supported by evidence. The other two flags about MCP are unsupported by the provided evidence.

revised.name: Google Ads
revised.one_liner: Google Ads API provides broad REST/gRPC access to campaign management and reporting via OAuth2 + developer-token auth, free to use but gated by a manager-account developer token application and access-level approval.

auth_methods.notes: Requires OAuth 2.0 access token plus a developer-token header; supports service account, single-user, and multi-user OAuth workflows with scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/adwords. login-customer-id header required when using a manager account.

revised.self_serve_notes: API usage itself is free, but developers must obtain a developer token via a Google Ads manager account and apply for an access level (Basic/Standard) through the API Center; this involves a Google review/approval process (Required Minimum Functionality compliance) rather than instant self-serve API key generation.

api_surface.notes: Also gRPC/client libraries (Java, Python, etc.); GAQL query language for reporting; broad coverage of campaign, ad group, keyword, budget, and reporting resources.

revised.main_blocker: Developer token approval process (manager account setup + access level application) adds friction before an agent toolkit can call the live API.

evidence.supports: Developer token required, obtained via manager account
Access levels and RMF approval process for API access
API is free to use at all access levels
